note: Rigged greybox trooper + animation clips (idle/walk/fire/death), generated by tools/models/gen_trooper_rig.py (decisions.md D41). The `.glb` is the interchange SOURCE (real glTF animation channels on a rigid-part rig — each box part bound 1.0 to a single bone, no soft vertex skinning). The cooked `.skel` (GDSK) is what the engine actually loads at runtime (render::skel): a hand-parseable skeleton + per-frame baked armature-space TRS the renderer plays back by drawing each part at model = place * A_bone(t) * inverse_bind — the CP-3/WS-B animation floor. Regenerate with `pnpm assets:rig`. License-clean by construction (code-authored geometry + keyframes, CC0-1.0).

assets.name: trooper_rig
assets.description: Rigid-part greybox trooper (pelvis/torso/head/helmet/arms/legs/rifle) on a 7-bone hierarchy, carrying four clips: idle, walk, fire, death. The cooked `.skel` is consumed at runtime; the `.glb` is interchange source.
assets.author: Jared Howard
